claims.text: Mars's two moons Phobos and Deimos were discovered in 1877 by American astronomer Asaph Hall.

evidence.quote:
American astronomer asop Hall discovered
them in
1877

claims.rationale: Asaph Hall's August 1877 discovery of Phobos (Aug 18) and Deimos (Aug 12) at the US Naval Observatory is uncontroversial astronomical history.

claims.text: Phobos is hollow and has a thin sheet-metal outer shell, meaning it is an artificial alien construction.

evidence.quote:
schlowsky
hypothesized that Phobos must have a
thin sheet metal structure on its
outside

Phobos is
artificial unnatural alien whatever you
want to call it

claims.rationale: Shklovsky did publish the hollow-Phobos hypothesis in 1959 based on a miscalculated orbital-decay rate. Later improved density and orbit measurements (especially from Viking, Mars Express, and gravitational-field studies) have shown Phobos is a rubble-pile asteroid, not hollow and not metallic. The hypothesis was retracted by Shklovsky himself. Near-zero directTruth.

claims.text: Fred Singer, President Eisenhower's science advisor, endorsed the hollow-Phobos hypothesis as scientifically sound.

evidence.quote:
Fred singer wasn't a nobody he was
the science adviser to President
Eisenhower

claims.rationale: S. Fred Singer did publish a 1960 Astronautics article discussing Shklovsky's hollow-Phobos idea as a legitimate alternative given the then-unexplained orbital anomaly. He served in various science advisory roles in the Eisenhower era, though 'science adviser to Eisenhower' slightly overstates his formal position (he was director of Weather Satellite Service). Medium-high directTruth for the core claim.

dependencies.rationale: Singer's endorsement is cited as credibility for the hollow-Phobos hypothesis.

claims.text: The Soviet Union launched Phobos 1 and Phobos 2 probes in July 1988 to study Mars's moon Phobos.

evidence.quote:
in July 1988 the Soviets launched two
probes Phobos 1 and Phobos 2

claims.rationale: The Soviet Phobos program with probes 1 (launched July 7, 1988) and 2 (launched July 12, 1988) is well-documented space-exploration history.

claims.text: On March 28, 1989 Phobos 2 lost contact after malfunctioning and sent one final image of a massive elliptical UFO that had attacked it.

evidence.quote:
on March 28 1989 just as
Phobos 2 Was preparing to make its
closest approach to Phobos something
unexpected happened

fobos 2 was
attacked by a

a long elliptical shadow cast
on the surface
Phobos a massive oval object w

claims.rationale: Phobos 2 did fail on March 27-28, 1989. The 'attacked by UFO' framing is the speculative layer; the probe's tumbling spin and telemetry loss were later attributed by Soviet engineers (and corroborated in a 1989 Nature paper) to an attitude-control computer fault. The 'elliptical object' was ultimately identified as Phobos's own shadow on Mars plus an infrared-camera calibration artifact - the host himself debunks this later.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] The host's own debunking directly rejects the UFO attack framing.

claims.text: The Russian government classified the final four images from Phobos 2 to cover up evidence of an alien encounter.

evidence.quote:
the Russians kept the last four
frames of the photo dump classified

claims.rationale: The host himself later reveals that 57 additional images were actually sent back after the alleged 'last' image - contradicting the cover-up framing. The Russian classification narrative originates with Marina Popovich and UFO-community secondary sources, not primary Soviet archives. Low directTruth.

claims.text: Soviet test pilot Marina Popovich was a whistleblower who in 1991 revealed classified Phobos 2 images showing a 15-mile UFO approaching the moon.

evidence.quote:
in 1991 Soviet test pilot Maria
papovich revealed one of those top
secret Russian photos

claims.rationale: The host himself explicitly denies Popovich was a whistleblower - she had official Soviet permission to show the photos, and she was later buried with full military honors, inconsistent with whistleblower persecution. Her UFO-believer bias was well-known. Low directTruth for the whistleblower / cover-up framing.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Host's verdict-section explicitly rejects the whistleblower narrative.

claims.text: Phobos has a 279-foot by 300-foot rectangular monolith, possibly an alien power substation harnessing zero-point energy.

evidence.quote:
only a short distance from the giant
crater is the Phobos Monolith

t's 279
ft across and 300 ft tall It's oddly
bright and oddly rectangular nature
like

claims.rationale: The 'Phobos Monolith' is a real but mundane boulder imaged by Mars Global Surveyor (2001), about 85m across. Dimensions are reasonably close to reported. Similar isolated boulders appear on our Moon and on asteroids, consistent with impact ejecta. The zero-point-energy-substation reading has no scientific basis - zero-point energy cannot be tapped as a power source under current physics.

claims.text: Buzz Aldrin confirmed the Phobos monolith is evidence of artificial construction by an unknown intelligence.

evidence.quote:
but if you listen to the full CLI he
thinks it's a natural formation

claims.rationale: Host explicitly denies this: the famous 'who put that there' Aldrin clip is typically cut to imply alien origin, but the full clip shows Aldrin attributing the monolith to natural processes or 'the universe' / God. Aldrin never endorsed an alien-construction claim. Low directTruth.

claims.text: Phobos's deep parallel grooves are evidence of artificial engineering rather than natural processes.

evidence.quote: Phobos has deep uniform grooves

the grooves are probably
caused by Mars's gravity

claims.rationale: A 2024 Planetary Science Journal paper (Ramsley & Head) models the grooves as tidal-stress fractures from Mars's gravity pulling Phobos apart - exactly the natural explanation the host cites. The grooves are also not uniform on close inspection (varying widths and orientations). Low directTruth for the artificial-engineering framing.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Natural-tidal-stress explanation eliminates need for artificial-construction framing.

claims.text: The 'cigar-shaped UFO' image from Phobos 2 was a mundane infrared camera lens glitch, not an alien craft.

evidence.quote:
okay so there was no cigar shape UFO
that was a glitch with the infrared lens

claims.rationale: This is the host's explicit debunk: the white-line-down-every-frame pattern (which appeared even when Phobos 2 imaged Jupiter) is an infrared sensor defect, not an object. Non-infrared images from the same sequence show no object. This is the scientifically-accepted explanation.

claims.text: Phobos is not hollow but a rubble-pile formed when small asteroids clumped together, containing caverns rather than a shell.

evidence.quote:
Phobos was
formed when a bunch of small asteroids
Clump together

claims.rationale: The rubble-pile model for Phobos is the current scientific consensus, supported by its low density (~1.87 g/cc), irregular shape, and radar-bright internal porosity. Shklovsky's hollow hypothesis was based on an orbital-decay error since corrected. This is the verdict-section explanation.

claims.text: Phobos is tidally decaying toward Mars and will be pulled apart in roughly 40 million years.

evidence.quote:
the grooves are probably
caused by Mars's gravity

claims.rationale: Phobos's orbital decay and eventual tidal disruption or impact in ~30-50 Myr is established planetary science, based on well-measured spiral-in rate (~2 cm/year) and tidal-stress modeling. The 40-Myr figure is within the accepted range.

claims.text: Japan's JAXA is sending a Mars Moon eXploration probe in September to collect samples from Phobos.

evidence.quote:
this
September Japan is sending a probe to
Mars and that probe is scheduled to
coll

claims.rationale: JAXA's MMX (Martian Moons eXploration) mission is a real, publicly-announced program aimed at sample-return from Phobos. Its launch window has slipped from originally-planned 2024 to 2026, but the mission exists and the overall claim is accurate.

claims.text: Monoliths like the one on Phobos are common natural features across the solar system including on our own Moon.

evidence.quote:
yes but
it's not an unusual feature monoliths
like this are found all over the solar
system including our moon

claims.rationale: Isolated tall boulders produced by impact ejecta are genuinely common on low-gravity bodies with regolith (small moons, asteroids). The lunar 'monoliths' referenced are Apollo and LRO imagery of natural boulders. Supported by planetary-geology literature.

dependencies.rationale: [alternative] If monoliths are common natural ejecta, the Phobos one needs no alien explanation.
